bullets.text: Iran reportedly names Mojtaba Khamenei as new supreme leader, raising succession, legitimacy, and policy-direction uncertainty amid war.
U.S. Tomahawk strike hit Iranian naval base beside a school; collateral-damage allegations could intensify escalation and diplomatic backlash.
Oil surges above $110; market pricing in supply disruption risk and potential further spikes if Gulf shipping or infrastructure is hit.
War disruptions could push oil to $150–$200, implying higher inflation risk and potential earnings pressure across energy-intensive sectors.
South Korea’s Kospi falls over 8% and triggers a circuit breaker as broader Asia sells off on oil shock and geopolitical risk-off.
China CPI hits a three-year high while producer deflation eases, complicating stimulus: weaker disinflation tailwind and tougher policy tradeoffs.
G7 meeting on Iran framed as diplomacy fraying; coordinated sanctions, energy releases, and security commitments may shift global risk premia.
Trump pushes federal leverage over elections, targeting key states; heightened governance uncertainty could affect institutional trust and market sentiment.
Trump vows legislative blockade until voter-ID bill passes, raising shutdown and fiscal-brinkmanship risk alongside election-rule federalization pressures.
Stablecoins are accumulating large Treasury holdings, tying crypto liquidity to U.S. funding markets and creating potential run dynamics affecting short-term rates.
Trump executive order reportedly shields glyphosate and white phosphorus; signals deregulatory and defense-policy tilt with litigation, environmental, and humanitarian implications.
Satellites can detect subtle bridge deformation, enabling earlier maintenance prioritization and potentially lowering infrastructure failure risk and lifecycle costs.
Brain imaging clarifies how ketamine rapidly alleviates severe depression, supporting biomarker-driven treatment strategies and accelerating fast-acting antidepressant development.
New psilocybin-inspired drug may treat depression without hallucinations, potentially broadening adoption and improving regulatory and reimbursement pathways for psychedelic-adjacent therapies.
Scientists warn fake research is spreading faster than real science, increasing replication risk and potentially distorting funding, policy, and clinical decision-making.
